# Prompt Journal — {PROJECT_NAME}

## Purpose
- Keep the user prompt history and agent step journal in one append-only review trail.
- Make it easy to reopen what was asked, what changed, what was found, and what should happen next.
- If the work also includes agent-to-agent handoffs or conversation turns, pair this file with `agent-transcript.md`.

## Entry format
- Timestamp: short local date and time
- Prompt history: the user prompt, route, and short outcome
- Agent journal: the step, expectation, finding, improvement, and short evaluation
- Review path: the docs or artifacts the user should open later

## Prompt history
<!-- prompt-history-append -->

## Agent journal
<!-- agent-journal-append -->

## Review path
- Open this file when you want to review what was asked and how the work progressed.
- Keep entries short and append-only.
- Link to any supporting docs, validation output, or artifacts inside the entry itself.
- Use `agent-transcript.md` for task assignments and conversation turns between agents.
